primary_risks: If exported CSVs are not produced from the same workbook generation logic, they could diverge-ensure exports are generated immediately after workbook creation from the same run.
Minor labeling changes (sensitivity notation) might require updates to any formula text that includes the constant; validate formulas after changes.
validation_steps_post_revision: Open and inspect formula_manifest to confirm each listed formula matches the corresponding workbook cell expression.
Compare computed_gates.csv values to in-workbook displayed values (manual open) or to re-export from the revised run to ensure consistency.
Run verifier precheck: ensure artifact previews now include plain-text manifest/CSV so automated graders can verify semantics without openpyxl.

planned_changes.description: Add a formula manifest as a new sheet 'formula_manifest' in substrate_warpage_recipe.xlsx and export as formula_manifest.txt. For each key computed cell (evidence_table key columns and recipe summary cells), list the exact formula expression and the source cell ranges it references.

Export computed snapshot CSVs: (a) evidence_table_snapshot.csv with per-record computed booleans/values; (b) computed_gates.csv containing summary cells (records_outside_limit_count, needs_review_count, hard_threshold_check, budget_feasible flag, recipe_and_checks summary outputs).

Unify sensitivity_shift_pct notation across all artifacts and in-workbook labels to '0.05 (5%)' or choose one representation (prefer 0.05 in machine-readable fields and '5%' in human-readable text). Update process_change.md and workbook cell labels accordingly and add a short note on units.

Add README_verification.txt describing how to verify outputs without openpyxl: point to formula_manifest, the CSV snapshots, and list any optional libraries. State that the computed CSVs reflect the workbook's evaluated outputs for the embedded packet.
In process_change.md, add a short section 'Verification artifacts included' listing the manifest and CSVs so graders understand semantic claims are substantiated by the exported computed values.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Close verifier-programmatic verification gaps and remove notation ambiguity so automated graders/verifiers can confirm workbook formulas and computed outputs without requiring openpyxl or additional environment dependencies.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline submission meets the artifact contract and documents rule logic and recommendations clearly. Verification gaps remain because the workbook (.xlsx) could not be programmatically parsed in the verifier environment (openpyxl unavailable). The model grader assigned full semantic correctness despite the inability to inspect workbook formulas, and process_change.md shows a percent-vs-fraction notation inconsistency for sensitivity_shift_pct (lists '5' and '5%'; actual_outputs policy uses 0.05).
